name: REVERSE rapamycin and ProLon trial is active with no posted results
summary: REVERSE is direct Rapalogs trial-watch context but cannot support efficacy because it is active not recruiting with no posted results and combines rapamycin with fasting-mimicking diet context.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT07354620 as an active-not-recruiting 52-participant early phase 1 aging-prevention pilot testing weekly rapamycin and ProLon fasting-mimicking diet arms, with OMICm methylation-age, cognition, insulin-resistance, and safety endpoints and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Adults aged 50 to 80 years in an aging-prevention registry
time_horizon: Twenty-four-week methylation and cognition endpoints with twenty-eight-week safety follow-up
quantitative_note: Actual enrollment is 52; estimated primary completion was 2026-02-01; estimated study completion was 2026-05-01; results were not posted.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of benefit or harm.
The ProLon fasting-mimicking diet arm and combination context make rapamycin-specific attribution difficult.
Methylation-age movement would remain a biomarker signal unless paired with durable functional outcomes.
